entries.title: Gift Finder: know exactly what to get a friend
entries.body: Share your Wishlist with a friend using a simple code — they paste it in, tell us their budget, and get the perfect gift pick automatically, optimized to maximize how much you'll love it within what they want to spend. You can do the same for them: paste their code, set your budget, and see your best options ranked, with a tracker so you never forget what you've already gifted them. Find it from the "More" menu or the command palette.

entries.title: You vs. The Average Gamer: see your Gamer Index
entries.body: There's a new "You vs. The Average Gamer" panel in Stats that scores you across 9 categories — cost per hour, completion rate, backlog size, hours played per week, average rating, genre diversity, yearly spend, how fast you start games after buying them, and session length. Each one shows your number stacked right against a typical gamer's, with a verdict on whether you're ahead, behind, or right on par. It all rolls up into a single "Gamer Index" score (0-100) with a fun label like Elite Gamer or Your Own Lane, plus a callout for your single strongest edge and your biggest gap.

entries.title: Weekly Plan Autopilot: let your queue schedule itself
entries.body: Tell it how many hours you've got this week and Weekly Plan Autopilot builds you a day-by-day schedule, picking which game goes on which day based on how well it matches your mood, how long it's been waiting in your queue, and which days you actually tend to play. Check off sessions as you finish them and watch your weekly adherence score build up over time.

entries.title: Today tab now nudges you toward replays and queue guilt
entries.body: The Today tab has two new daily nudges. If you've got a dormant game worth revisiting, you'll see a Replay Radar suggestion right at the top — tap it to jump straight into the full Replay Radar view. And if something's been sitting in your Up Next queue getting embarrassingly old, you'll see it called out by name with its shame tier, with a tap straight through to your queue.

entries.title: Game Night: find what you and a friend can co-op tonight
entries.body: Want to play something together with a friend but never know what you both actually own? Try Game Night. Generate your code, send it to a friend, paste theirs back, and instantly see every game you both own, a compatibility score, and your shared genres. Then hit "Spin" to randomly pick tonight's game from everything you haven't both already finished — like a slot machine for game night. Find it from the "More" menu or the command palette.

entries.title: Time Capsule: write a note to future-you
entries.body: There's a new way to leave yourself a message: seal a prediction or note in a Time Capsule, pick a date for it to reopen (a month, a few months, even a year out), and tag a few games you want to keep an eye on. When the date arrives, you'll see a banner letting you know it's ready, and opening it up shows your original note side-by-side with what actually happened to those games — how many more hours you put in, whether you finished them, all of it. Find it from the "More" menu or the command palette.

entries.title: New "Today" tab: your daily gaming dashboard
entries.body: There's a brand new "Today" tab (look for the home icon, first in the row) that pulls together everything worth checking each day in one place: a friendly greeting, your current activity vibe, a one-tap "Play Tonight" button, your most important alert if you have one, your daily fortune cookie, On This Day memories, and your Daily Quests — all in one spot instead of scattered across every tab. The other tabs are cleaner now too, since those widgets used to show up everywhere.

entries.title: Stats History: watch your numbers move over time
entries.body: Your Stats tab now keeps a running history instead of just showing today's snapshot. There's a new "Stats History" chart where you can flip between Credit Score, Cost/Hour, Completion %, Total Hours, and Library Size, pick a 30-day, 90-day, or all-time window, and see exactly how each one has trended — plus a quick headline like "Credit Score rose from 612 to 776 over the last 30d." It quietly logs a snapshot once a day, so check back over the next few days and you'll start seeing your own trend lines take shape.

entries.title: You vs. The Critics: see how your ratings stack up against Metacritic
entries.body: There's a new card in your Stats tab comparing every game you've rated against its Metacritic score. It'll tell you whether you're a "Mainstream Taste" who mostly agrees with critics, a "True Contrarian" who loves what they hate, a "Tough Crowd," or somewhere in between — plus it calls out your single biggest hidden gem (a game you loved that critics slept on) and the one critically-adored game you just didn't buy into. Tap to expand and see the full breakdown for every game.

entries.title: Daily Quests now celebrate Perfect Days and show your streak as a strip
entries.body: Go 3-for-3 on your Daily Quests and you'll now get a little celebration toast right when the last quest checks off — no need to reopen the panel to find out you nailed it. Inside the expanded quest panel there's also a new "Last 7 days" strip: a row of small squares showing which of the past week's days you completed (green = perfect, amber = partial), so your streak isn't just a number anymore, it's a visible run of good days.

entries.title: Daily Quests: 3 small challenges, picked just for you, every day
entries.body: There's a new "Daily Quests" chip right below your Fortune Cookie. Every day it picks 3 quick challenges out of a pool — things like logging any session, hitting 90 minutes of play, switching genres, starting something from your backlog, playing whatever's queued up in Up Next, finishing a game, or reviving something you haven't touched in 3+ weeks. It only ever picks quests you can actually complete with your current library, so it never asks for something impossible. Tap the chip to see the full list with live checkmarks as you complete them, and keep your streak going by going 3-for-3 — miss a day and the streak resets, just like any good habit tracker.

entries.title: Calendar Sync: put your gaming plans on your real calendar
entries.body: Open ⋮ → "Calendar Sync" (or find it with ⌘K) to export your gaming plans to Apple Calendar, Outlook, Google Calendar, or anything else. It pulls together your Up Next play schedule, upcoming Buy Queue release dates, and your active goal deadlines, lets you pick exactly which ones you want, and either downloads a single calendar file with everything in it or drops a one-tap "Add to Google Calendar" link next to each item. No more trying to remember when a game releases or when you told yourself you'd finish your goal — it's right there next to your other plans.

entries.title: Press ⌘K to jump anywhere, instantly
entries.body: There's now a real search bar for the whole app. Press ⌘K (or Ctrl+K on Windows) anywhere, or tap the new search icon in the header, and start typing — it'll find any game in your library by name, jump straight to any tab, or fire off any of the ~20 actions that used to be buried in menus (Play Tonight, Random Pick, Gamer Card, Yearly Wrapped, Export, Steam Sync, Wishlist Planner, Replay Radar, and more). Use the arrow keys and Enter to pick a result, or just click it. Your most recently used commands show up first the next time you open it, so the things you reach for most are always one keystroke away.

entries.title: Your daily fortune now knows about your backlog and your wishlist
entries.body: The Daily Fortune Cookie can now serve up two new kinds of fortunes: a nudge toward your single best Replay Radar pick ("Abandoned 645 days ago — might hit different now"), and a heads-up on the next wishlist game that becomes affordable under your budget plan ("becomes affordable Sep 2026"). Same tap-to-crack cookie, just a little smarter about what's worth your attention today.

entries.title: Made a mistake? Undo it.
entries.body: Deleting a game no longer asks you to confirm first — it just deletes, and a quick toast pops up with an "Undo" button for a few seconds afterward so you can bring it right back if you tapped the wrong one. The same safety net now covers marking a game Abandoned in Backlog Triage and finishing a bulk import or Steam sync, so a misclick or a bad import never has to be permanent.

entries.title: Replay Radar: a ranked list of games worth playing again
entries.body: Open ⋮ → "Replay Radar" to see which dormant games in your library actually deserve another shot. It looks at everything you haven't touched in 3+ weeks and ranks it by how highly you rated it, how long it's been sitting, how good the value already is, and whether you left it unfinished — sorting the results into Overdue, Ready, Worth It, and Someday tiers with a one-line reason for each pick, like "One of your highest-rated games — 645 days since you last touched it." Tap any game to jump straight into its detail view.

entries.title: Wishlist Planner: find out exactly when you can afford what you want
entries.body: Open ⋮ → "Wishlist Planner" to see when each game on your wishlist actually fits your budget. Set a yearly budget once, then drag-free reorder your wishlist with up/down arrows to choose what you're saving up for first — the planner does the math and tells you the month each one becomes affordable, plus when your whole list will be cleared. Your priority order is remembered, so it's right there next time you open it.

entries.title: Squad Leaderboard: rank your whole friend group, not just one rival
entries.body: Rival Check now has a "Squad" tab. Paste in codes from several friends at once and see everyone ranked together on one leaderboard — a composite score blends all 7 stats (hours, library size, spend, cost-per-hour, rating, completion rate, credit score), with a crown for whoever's on top and "Best in Squad" highlights for whoever wins each individual stat. Your saved squad sticks around the next time you open it, so you don't have to re-paste everyone's code every time.

entries.title: Rival Check: compare libraries with a friend, no accounts needed
entries.body: Open ⋮ → "Rival Check" to challenge a friend to a head-to-head. You get a short text code that packs up your library stats — total hours, spend, cost-per-hour, average rating, completion rate, and your Gaming Credit Score — with nothing about your actual game list inside it. Send it to a friend any way you like (text, Discord, whatever), and when they send theirs back, paste it in to see a full stat-by-stat comparison with a winner called for each category and an overall verdict. No sign-in, no server, no shared account required.

entries.title: Backlog Bracket: a tournament for "what should I play next?"
entries.body: Can't decide what to play next? Open ⋮ → "Backlog Bracket" to seed a single-elimination tournament from your backlog — weighted toward the games that have been waiting longest — and just tap your way to a winner. Pick a bracket size (4, 8, or 16 games), choose a winner in each head-to-head matchup, and a champion gets crowned in a few taps. From there you can start playing it immediately or send it to your Up Next queue. Past champions are saved in a "Hall of Champions" so you can see what's won before.

entries.title: Achievement Hunter: see your real Steam achievement progress
entries.body: If you've synced Steam, open ⋮ → "Achievement Hunter" to see exactly how close you are to 100% on every game — total achievements unlocked, how many games you've fully completed, and your single rarest unlock across your whole library (with the percentage of players who have it too). Tap any game to see every achievement, unlocked or not, with icons and descriptions. No extra setup needed — it reuses your existing Steam sync.

entries.title: Sync your real Steam library in one tap
entries.body: No more typing in every game by hand. Open ⋮ → "Sync Steam Library", paste a free Steam API key and your SteamID, and we'll pull your entire owned-games list straight from Steam — with your real playtime included. Preview every game before importing, with already-owned and already-synced titles unchecked for you automatically. Your Steam key is stored only on this device and never leaves it except to talk to Steam directly.

entries.title: Alerts now remember anniversaries and almost-earned trophies
entries.body: The bell icon in the header just got smarter. It will now nudge you about gaming anniversaries ("1 year ago you started Elden Ring") and let you know when you are within a few percent of leveling up a trophy ("92% to Gold Century Club"). And if any alert category is not your thing, tap the new settings gear inside the alerts panel to mute it for good — budget warnings, queue reminders, anniversaries, whatever you want quiet.

entries.title: Genre level-ups now get a celebration
entries.body: Push a genre to a new level — or a whole new rank like Adept or Master — and you'll see a toast pop up celebrating it, with a crown if it's your Main Class. Your Gamer Card (the shareable one) now shows your Main Class badge too, right next to your gamer personality. Also fixed a bug where trophy-earned toasts were silently never showing up — those now fire properly alongside the new genre ones.

entries.title: Time Machine: undo accidental deletes and bad imports
entries.body: Your library now quietly saves itself in the background, and you can save a snapshot manually any time too. Open "Time Machine" from the ⋮ menu to see every snapshot with exactly what's changed since — games added, removed, or edited — and restore any of them with one tap. Accidentally deleted a game? A bulk import went sideways? Just roll back to right before it happened. You'll never lose your library to a mistake again.

entries.title: Level up your favorite genres
entries.body: Every genre you play is now its own character class that levels up the more you play it — hours, finishing games, and rating them all earn XP. Head to the Stats tab to see your rank in each genre (Novice through Legend), your "Main Class" — the genre that defines you as a gamer — and what perks you unlock as you level up. It's a fun new way to see where your gaming energy actually goes, and which genre you've truly mastered versus which ones you just dabble in.

entries.title: Price drops in your buy queue now show up as alerts
entries.body: If a game in your Buy Queue hits the target price you set, or drops to a new low even before reaching that target, it now surfaces in the alerts bell up top — no need to keep checking back manually. There's also a new "Check all prices" button on the Buy Queue tab that refreshes every game's price in one tap, instead of checking each one individually. Tapping a price alert jumps you straight to the Buy Queue.

entries.title: A new bell icon watches your budget, queue, and backlog for you
entries.body: There's now a bell icon up top that quietly keeps an eye on the stuff you'd hate to miss: getting close to (or over) your yearly budget, a game that's been sitting shamefully long in your Up Next queue, a backlog game about to expire, a goal deadline coming up fast, or a play session that's been running for hours. Tap the bell to see everything at a glance, dismiss what you've handled, snooze the rest for a day, or turn on desktop notifications so the important ones reach you even with the tab closed.

entries.title: Start a live timer while you play
entries.body: Hit "Timer" on any game card (or the "Start Live Timer" button in a game's detail sheet) and a Now Playing bar drops in at the bottom of the screen with a running stopwatch — it keeps counting even if you switch tabs or come back later. Pause it, resume it, or tap stop to log the session: the hours are pre-filled from the timer, and you can tag your mood (🔥 Great, 👍 Good, 😐 Meh, 💪 Grind) before saving. No more guessing how long you actually played.

entries.title: See your budget impact before you hit save
entries.body: Set a yearly budget on the Stats tab? Now when you add (or edit) a game's price in the Add Game form, you'll see exactly what it does to your budget — money spent so far this year, what's left after this purchase, and a color-coded bar that turns amber as you approach your limit and red if you'd go over. No more finding out you blew the budget after the fact.

entries.title: Import your library from a CSV or JSON file
entries.body: Got a spreadsheet of games, or an old export from this app? Tap ⋮ then "Import games" to bring it in. Drop in a file or paste CSV text, review a preview of what was found (games already in your library are flagged so you don't accidentally duplicate them), uncheck anything you don't want, and import the rest in one go. Pairs with the existing Export so you've got a full backup-and-restore loop.

entries.title: Solo or social? Find out where your hours actually go
entries.body: When logging a play session, you can now tag who you played with — Solo, Co-op, Online, Couch Co-op, or Streamed. The Stats tab's Analytics panel turns those tags into a Social Gaming Breakdown: a solo-vs-social split of your hours, a per-mode bar, and a quick insight like your go-to co-op game. Tag a few sessions to unlock it.

entries.title: Backlog Triage — make peace with your pile of unplayed games
entries.body: Tap ⋮ then "Backlog Triage" to swipe through the games in your library that actually need a decision — the ones going stale unstarted, or stalled mid-playthrough. One card at a time, you choose: Queue it up next, Let it go (mark abandoned), or Snooze for three weeks. A summary at the end tallies how many hours and dollars you finally made a call on. No more scrolling past the same forgotten games every time you open the app.

entries.title: Set goals and track your progress
entries.body: A new Gaming Goals panel on the Stats tab lets you set personal challenges — finish a number of games, stick to a spending limit, hit an hours target, branch into new genres, clear your backlog, or track any custom metric you like. Watch your progress bar fill in real time, and look back at past goals in a collapsible history once they're done.

entries.title: Play Tonight — find your perfect match in seconds
entries.body: Tap ⋮ then "Play Tonight" and tell the app how much time you have — Quick, Standard, Long, or Marathon. It instantly ranks your unfinished games by chemistry, session-length fit, and current momentum, showing your top pick with a highlighted card and a one-line reason, plus four runner-ups. Tap any result to jump straight into the game's detail sheet and log your session.

entries.title: Compare any two games head to head
entries.body: Open any game's detail sheet, tap the new ⇄ Compare button, and pick a second game from your library. You'll see a full side-by-side breakdown — price, hours, rating, cost-per-hour, ROI, sessions — with a winner highlighted for each category, a radar chart visualising both profiles at once, and an overall verdict showing which game came out on top.

entries.title: What would your library look like with better choices?
entries.body: The new Alternate Reality Simulator in the Stats tab lets you explore four "what if" scenarios: what if you'd skipped impulse buys, never bought games you later abandoned, cleared your backlog, or always bought on sale? Each scenario shows the exact games affected, a before/after stat comparison, and a frank insight about your spending habits.

entries.title: Search your library instantly
entries.body: A search bar now lives at the top of the Games tab. Type a game name, genre, or platform and your list narrows in real time. Works great when you have a big library and just want to find that one game quickly.

entries.title: Updates live here now
entries.body: This is the new What's New panel. When the app changes, you'll see a friendly summary here so you always know what's different.
